title: Tanker report - Week 33
summary:
VLCC
The week began slowly in the Middle East to US Gulf (routing via the Cape/Cape) remaining in the WS18-18.5 region, while MEG to China is valued at the WS31.32 level (showing a round-trip TCE of $-3164/day). In the Atlantic, rates for 260,000mt West Africa to China are again at around the WS33 mark (a TCE of $-265/day round trip) and 270,000mt US Gulf to China saw another drop of $68.75k to $3.9125m (a roundtrip TCE of $1.696k/day). Suezmax
In West Africa there were a few pieces of enquiry, which caused a modest 1.22 point improvement in rates for the 130,000mt Nigeria/UK Continent market to the WS55.45 level (a round trip TCE of $3.7k/day).

children.title: VLCC
children.summary: The week began slowly in the Middle East to US Gulf (routing via the Cape/Cape) remaining in the WS18-18.5 region, while MEG to China is valued at the WS31.32 level (showing a round-trip TCE of $-3164/day). In the Atlantic, rates for 260,000mt West Africa to China are again at around the WS33 mark (a TCE of $-265/day round trip) and 270,000mt US Gulf to China saw another drop of $68.75k to $3.9125m (a roundtrip TCE of $1.696k/day).

children.title: Suezmax
children.summary: In West Africa there were a few pieces of enquiry, which caused a modest 1.22 point improvement in rates for the 130,000mt Nigeria/UK Continent market to the WS55.45 level (a round trip TCE of $3.7k/day). Rates for 135,000mt Black Sea/Med also rose 3.7s points to WS62.61 (a round-trip TCE of about $-2.9k/day).

children.title: Aframax
children.summary: In the Mediterranean, the market for 80,000mt Ceyhan/Lavera remains stable thus far at the WS87.5 mark (showing a round-trip TCE of $2.9k/day). In Northern Europe the market for 80,000mt Cross-North Sea saw some softening of rates and is now at the WS87.5 level (a round trip TCE of $-5.9k/day) while in the 100,000mt Baltic/UK Continent market rates eased by 0.94 points to WS57.19 (a round trip TCE of $-2.4k/day).

children.title: Clean
children.summary: The Middle East Gulf has seen softening sentiment this week. On the LR2s to Japan TC1 dropped 11.25 points to WS92.5, a round-trip TCE of $6,826/day.
